zh.lifeSummary: Eve de Broc是Eustace de Broc的女兒，於大約1090年出生在英國英格蘭素可安莫林。她於大約1136年與Baron Walter de Chesney在英國諾福爾可霍斯福德結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾霍卡斯特爾。

nameMeanings.name: Broc
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and North German: variant of   Brook  .

English and Scandinavian: nickname for a person supposedly resembling a badger, from Middle English brok(ke) ‘badger’ (Old English brocc) and Danish brok (a word of Celtic origin; compare Welsh broch, Cornish brogh, Irish broc). In the Middle Ages badgers were regarded as unpleasant creatures.
Dutch and Flemish: from a personal name, a short form of   Brockert  .

variants.name: Brockel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gottlieb, Kurt.
in northern Germany, a habitational name from Bröckel near Celle or from Brökeln on the river Weser.

variants.name: Broich
meanings: German: from northwest German topographic names and placenames, see   Brock   6.

variants.name: Brook
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived by a brook, stream, water-meadows or low, marshy ground, from Middle English brook, Old English brōc ‘brook, stream’; or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, such as Brook (Kent, Rutland) or Brooke (Norfolk).
North German: topographic name for someone who lived by a water meadow or marsh, from Low German brook (cognate with German Bruch ‘swamp’ and Old English brōc; see 1 above).
Americanized form of Dutch Broek: topographic name from broek, a cognate of 2 above.

variants.name: Broxterman
meanings: Americanized form of German Broxtermann: topographic name from Brock , for someone living by a stream, or a habitational name for someone from Broxten near Osnabrück, named with the same word.

variants.name: De Brock
meanings: Dutch and Flemish: variant of Brock as a topographic name meaning ‘swampy land’, with the definite article de ‘the’.

nameMeanings.name: Eve
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: vernacular form of the name borne in the Bible by the first woman, created from one of Adam's ribs (Genesis 2:22). It derives, via Latin Eva, from Hebrew Havva, which is considered to be a variant of the vocabulary word hayya ‘living’ or ‘animal’. Adam gave names to all the animals (Genesis 2:19–20) and then to his wife, who was ‘the mother of all living’ (Genesis 3:20).

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of any of the given names beginning with the letter D- ( compare Jay and Kay ), in particular Dorothy . It is also used as an independent name, and may in some cases be associated with the River Dee ( compare Clyde ).

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: Eve de Broc was born about 1090, in Angmering, Sussex, England, United Kingdom as the daughter of Eustace de Broc. She married Baron Walter de Chesney about 1136, in Horsford, Norfolk, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. She died in Horncastle, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom.

Elle a épousé Baron Walter de Chesney environ 1136, à Horsford, Norfolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée à Horncastle, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Eve de Brocは約1090年にイギリスイギリススセックスアングメリングでEustace de Brocの娘として生まれた。約1136年にイギリスノーフォルクホールスフォードでBaron Walter de Chesneyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女はイギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアホールンカストルで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Eve de Broc(은)는 대략 1090년 영국 잉글랜드 수세스 앤그머잉에서 Eustace de Broc의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1136년 영국 노르폴크 호르스퍼드에서 Baron Walter de Chesney(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 호른카스틀에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Eve de Broc родилась около 1090 г. в местности ангмиринг, суссекс, Англия, Великобритания. Её отца звали Eustace de Broc. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Baron Walter de Chesney около 1136 г. в местности хаурсфорд, норфолк, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла в местности хаурнкастл, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания.
